overview: Amulet Titan is a skill-intensive Modern strategy that combines explosive ramp with a toolbox-style land package and intricate sequencing. It aims to deploy Primeval Titan as early as turn two to snowball into a winning position, while maintaining enough interaction and redundancy to grind through disruption in longer games.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with Amulet of Vigor + 1-2 lands, or 2+ lands + 1+ mana dork (Elvish Mystic, Llanowar Elves, Birds of Paradise).
Keep hands with Scapeshift + 2+ lands if supported by ramp.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only Primeval Titan and no ramp/Amulet, or hands with multiple dorks but no lands.

early_game: Play Arboreal Grazer on turn 1 for a land and blocker
Sequence Amulet of Vigor and bounce lands carefully to dodge disruption
Use Explore and Spelunking to smooth land drops and draw cards
Turn 1: Play land, cast a mana dork.
Turn 2: Play second land, cast Amulet of Vigor (or second dork).
Tap dorks, untap with Amulet, play Primeval Titan or Scapeshift.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast combo pieces
prioritize dual lands if you have fetches.
mid_game: Deploy Primeval Titan to fetch lands that stabilize, protect, or attack
Use Summoner's Pact when you can win immediately or afford the upkeep cost
Leverage Urza's Saga to tutor Amulet or generate Construct token pressure
always prioritize untapping lands with Amulet.
Don't overcommit to one Titan if you can Scapeshift for more.
Keep protection cards in hand until you're ready to combo.
Graveyard is generally not a primary resource, but monitor opponents' graveyard strategies.
Hold back Primeval Titan if you have Scapeshift in hand and can combo off next turn.
late_game: Chain Cultivator Colossus with untapped lands to draw cards and grow the creature
Execute Aftermath Analyst loops with Scapeshift or Lotus Field for massive mana bursts
Utilize Shifting Woodland and Mirrorpool for infinite recursion and repeated Titan triggers
Scapeshift for 10+ lands (lethal), or Primeval Titan + Scapeshift for massive land search + lethal.
Sometimes just casting Primeval Titan and attacking with it if the board is open.
When you have Amulet + 2+ mana dorks + Primeval Titan/Scapeshift in hand, or when you can reliably cast Scapeshift for 10+ lands.
Also commit when you have protection (counterspell, discard, or instant-speed protection) and the opponent has no removal.

key_cards.name: Amulet of Vigor
key_cards.notes: Lets bounce lands function like supercharged ramp by untapping them before they return to hand.

key_cards.name: Primeval Titan
key_cards.notes: Fetches lands to ramp further, stabilize, or set up lethal damage.

key_cards.name: Simic Growth Chamber
key_cards.notes: Creates explosive mana when combined with Amulet of Vigor.

key_cards.name: Scapeshift
key_cards.notes: Sacrifices lands to bring in the exact package needed for lethal or utility.

key_cards.name: Spelunking
key_cards.notes: Draws a card, drops a land, and makes all future lands enter untapped.

key_cards.name: Urza's Saga
key_cards.notes: Tutors Amulet of Vigor and generates Construct tokens as a backup plan.

key_cards.name: Aftermath Analyst
key_cards.notes: Turns graveyard recursion into fuel by returning sacrificed lands untapped.

key_cards.name: Cultivator Colossus
key_cards.notes: Refills hand with extra lands while growing huge, especially with untapped land drops.

sequencing_tips: When Amulet of Vigor and a bounce land trigger, ensure Amulet's untap resolves before the land's return trigger to tap it for mana first.
Sequence Amulet of Vigor and lands carefully to dodge disruption; sometimes play Amulet first, sometimes lay lands out first.
Use Summoner's Pact only when you can win that turn or guarantee mana for the upkeep cost.
Float mana and plan ahead for Urza's Saga's Chapter III sacrifice timing.
Chain Cultivator Colossus land drops while Amulet or Spelunking is active to maximize card draw and creature size.
Amulet of Vigor is the engine
never mulligan it if you have lands.
Primeval Titan is both a threat and a win condition.
Watch for Stax effects (Wasteland, Field of Ruin) which can shut you down.

matchups.advice: Side in Fire Magic to sweep small creatures and Dismember for larger threats. Cut Arboreal Grazer since blocking is unimportant.
Bring in Six for long-game card advantage, Dismember for early creatures, and Bojuka Bog to shrink their graveyard. Trim Scapeshift and Explore.
Use Force of Vigor to break their setup and Collector Ouphe to lock down artifacts. Cut Cultivator Colossus and Arboreal Grazer.
Fight back quickly with Force of Vigor on Ruby Medallion, Dismember on cost reducers, and Bojuka Bog. Cut slow cards like Cultivator Colossus, Spelunking, and Explore.
Focus on keeping your engine intact. Force of Vigor is crucial for breaking their Amulet of Vigor.

Use discard (Thoughtseize) to remove counterspells. Use cheap, uncounterable threats if possible. Use Ponder/Preordain to dig for protection. Sometimes just casting Scapeshift in response to a counter if you have a backup plan, but generally, hate out or discard is key.
Use Stifle on their combo pieces. Use Red Elemental Blast or Abrupt Decay on their mana rocks. Attack early with Titan to race them. Don't let them get ahead in mana.
Use Lightning Helix/Go for the Throat to remove early threats. Use Primeval Titan to block or trade. Sometimes you can combo off turn 2 or 3 to win before they stabilize.
Pressure with early threats. Use discard to remove key control cards. Combo off before they can stabilize. Use Scapeshift to go wide and pressure.
It's a race. Use Thoughtseize to remove their Amulet or Scapeshift. Use discard to remove their protection. Attack with Titan to force trades. Sometimes you just combo off first.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Force of Will, Ponder/Preordain into countermagic), removal (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares), and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents play discard or hand disruption.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: The deck is one of the most powerful and skill-intensive strategies in Modern, one that combines explosive ramp with toolbox-style land packages and some of the most intricate sequencing in the format. At its best, this deck can land a Primeval Titan as early as turn 2, but it also has the staying power to grind through removal and disruption in longer games.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: The primary win condition is to overwhelm with Primeval Titan. It chains into damage by finding Hanweir Battlements for haste or lands like Lotus Field for mana explosions. Scapeshift is the alternate plan, which is to turn all your lands into a game-ending swing. When needed, Cultivator Colossus is big enough to trample over just about anything.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: When both triggers go on the stack, make sure Amulet’s untap resolves before the land’s “return a land” trigger... Sometimes you jam an Amulet first, other times you lay out your lands carefully to dodge disruption... You usually want to use it when you’re winning that same turn... Just be mindful of Saga’s timing: It sacrifices itself after Chapter III, so float mana and plan ahead.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Dismember gives you cheap creature removal, even against larger threats... clean up creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, Ledger Shredder, or Abhorrent Oculus... Force of Vigor handles problematic artifacts and enchantments... Bojuka Bog gives you some graveyard interaction.
